headline: ROTATION CONFIRMATION (first agent assessment). Live composite 65.6 (Established). Note: scanner rotation_state listed 68.6; using live data per assessor protocol — DATA INTEGRITY FLAG. HRW World Report 2026 evidence: Japan retains death penalty with execution-day notification practice (105 on death row); asylum/refugee determination strongly oriented against grants (190 of 12,373 applications recognized in 2024); foreign technical intern training program abolished in June and replaced with new training/employment system allowing eventual employer changes (positive structural reform but vague conditions per critics). High institutional capacity, strong rule of law, broadly stable democracy. Composite 65.6 (Established mid-band) appropriately reflects deep institutional capacity offset by sustained death-penalty + refugee/migrant policy concerns. No 5pt-threshold-crossing acute event. Composite holds.
data_integrity_flag: Scanner rotation_state listed Japan at 68.6; live data is 65.6. Used live per assessor protocol.

evidence.finding: Death penalty retained with execution-day notification practice; 105 on death row; one execution in 2025. Asylum recognition rate extremely low (190/12,373 in 2024). Diet abolished foreign technical intern training program in June, established new training/employment system with eventual employer-change rights but vague conditions.
Sustained documentation of death penalty practice, refugee determination concerns, gender-discrimination issues.

negativeSignals: Death penalty regime with execution-day notification; very low refugee recognition rate; lingering ambiguity in new migrant-worker framework; sustained gender-equality and minority-rights concerns.
positiveSignals: June 2025 abolition of technical intern program is structural positive INT/EMP signal; high institutional capacity; rule of law; stable democracy; wealthy developmental-aid contributor.
